content: What type of hybridisation is found in $\mathrm{H}_2 \mathrm{Se}$ ?
explanation:
Step 1: Determine the central atom and its valence electrons

  • Central atom: Se (selenium)

  • Electronic configuration of Se: [Ar] 3d¹⁰ 4s² 4p⁴

    → Total 6 valence electrons

Step 2: Bond formation

  • Selenium forms two single bonds with two hydrogen atoms.

  • Each hydrogen contributes one electron.

  • So, 2 electrons are paired for bonding with H → 2 bonds

  • Remaining = 6 − 2 = 4 electrons (2 lone pairs) remain on Se.

Thus, around the Se atom:

  • 2 bonding pairs

  • 2 lone pairs

→ Total 4 electron pairs

Step 3: Determine hybridisation

For 4 electron pairs → sp³ hybridisation.

Step 4: Geometry

  • Electron-pair geometry: Tetrahedral

  • Molecular shape (due to 2 lone pairs): Bent (angular), similar to H₂O.

✅ Answer: Option C — sp³ hybridisation

content: Identify the compound having highest value of bond angle from following.
explanation: The $\mathrm{BeCl}_2$ molecule has linear geometry with a $\mathrm{Cl}-\mathrm{Be}-\mathrm{Cl}$ bond angle of $180^{\circ}$.

content: Which of the following compounds has highest lattice energy?
explanation:
To determine which compound has the highest lattice energy, we use the lattice energy trend:

$ \text{Lattice energy} \propto \frac{Q_1 Q_2}{r} $

where

  • $Q_1$ and $Q_2$ are the ionic charges,

  • $r$ is the distance between the ions (sum of ionic radii).

Step 1: Charges

All given compounds are of the type MF, where M = alkali metal and F = fluoride ion.

  • $M^+ = \text{Na}^+, \text{K}^+, \text{Rb}^+, \text{Cs}^+$

  • $F^- = \text{constant anion}$

Charge on each = ±1 → So $Q_1 Q_2$ is the same for all.

Step 2: Size of cations

Cation size increases down the group:

$ \text{Na}^+ < \text{K}^+ < \text{Rb}^+ < \text{Cs}^+ $

Step 3: Lattice energy vs. ionic size

Smaller ions → shorter interionic distance → stronger attraction → higher lattice energy.

Therefore, NaF (with the smallest cation) has the highest lattice energy.

✅ Final Answer:

Option C: NaF

content: Which of the following is NOT an example of diamagnetic substance?
explanation:
Let's analyse each option:

Diamagnetic substances are those which have all electrons paired and are repelled by a magnetic field.

  • Option A: $\mathrm{N}_2$

    In the molecular orbital diagram, all electrons in $\mathrm{N}_2$ are paired.

    $\rightarrow$ Diamagnetic

  • Option B: $\mathrm{F}_2$

    $\mathrm{F}_2$ molecule also has all electrons paired in molecular orbitals.

    $\rightarrow$ Diamagnetic

  • Option C: NaCl

    Sodium chloride is an ionic solid. Both $\mathrm{Na}^+$ and $\mathrm{Cl}^-$ ions have all paired electrons.

    $\rightarrow$ Diamagnetic

  • Option D: Co (Cobalt)

    Atomic number of Co $= 27$

    Its electronic configuration:

    $[\mathrm{Ar}]\, 3d^7\, 4s^2$

    In the $3d^7$ configuration, there are unpaired electrons.

    $\rightarrow$ NOT diamagnetic (it is paramagnetic)

Final Answer:

Option D: Co is NOT an example of a diamagnetic substance.

content: What is the formal charge present on oxygen atom (numbered 1) in Lewis structure of $\mathrm{CO}_2$ ?
explanation: $$ \begin{aligned} &\text { Formal charge of oxygen atom (numbered 1) }\\ &\begin{aligned} & =V E-N E-\frac{1}{2}(B E) \\ & =6-2-\frac{1}{2}(6)=4-3=+1 \end{aligned} \end{aligned} $$
